name: Mashed Root Vegetables with Sweet Garlic (l A
recipeIngredient: 2 medium celery roots, peeled and cut into 2-inch chunks
2 large Russet potatoes, peeled and cut into 2-inch chunks
1 large parsnip, peeled and cut into 2-inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine celery root, potatoes, and parsnip in a pot of boiling salted water.
2. Cook for 10 minutes until vegetables are tender.
3. Add garlic cloves and cook for another 10 minutes.
4. Drain the vegetables well.
5. Transfer hot drained vegetables to a processor fitted with a metal blade.
6. Puree the mixture until very smooth, stopping occasionally to scrape down the sides of the bowl. This should take about 1 to 2 minutes.
7. Adjust seasonings to taste by adding salt, nutmeg, and pepper.
8. Pour the pureed vegetables into a greased 5-cup baking dish and cover with foil.
9. Refrigerate if making ahead or serve immediately at room temperature.
10. To serve, heat in an oven set to 300°F (150°C) until hot, or reheat in a microwave oven.
11. Spoon the hot mixture onto six heated dinner plates, dividing evenly.
12. Ladle venison ragout over the vegetables, dividing evenly.
13. Serve immediately.
This recipe serves 6 people and contains approximately:
- 147 calories per serving
- 416 mg sodium per serving
- 8 mg cholesterol per serving
- 3 grams fat per serving
- 28 grams carbohydrates per serving
- 4 grams protein per serving
- 2.21 grams fiber per serving
